edges.description: Loss of endothelin signaling impairs terminal migration of melanoblast and enteric neural crest precursors.
edges.source: EDN3 ligand and EDNRB receptor signaling deficiency
edges.target: Terminal migration defect of melanoblast and enteric precursors

edges.description: Failed melanoblast migration reduces melanocytes in pigmentary and auditory tissues.
edges.source: Terminal migration defect of melanoblast and enteric precursors
edges.target: Stria vascularis and cutaneous melanocyte deficiency

edges.description: Failed enteric precursor migration produces aganglionosis.
edges.source: Terminal migration defect of melanoblast and enteric precursors

edges.description: Stria vascularis melanocyte deficiency disrupts cochlear function and produces sensorineural hearing impairment.
edges.source: Stria vascularis and cutaneous melanocyte deficiency

edges.description: Cutaneous and ocular melanocyte deficiency produces the pigmentary abnormalities of Waardenburg-Shah syndrome.
edges.source: Stria vascularis and cutaneous melanocyte deficiency

edges.description: Enteric ganglion cell deficiency causes aganglionic megacolon.

edges.target: EDN3 ligand and EDNRB receptor signaling deficiency
EDN3 ligand and EDNRB receptor signaling deficiency

nodes.description: Hirschsprung disease results from failed enteric neural crest development and is the defining enteric component of Waardenburg-Shah syndrome.

evidence.snippet: WS type 4 (WS4), or Shah-Waardenburg syndrome, is also known as Hirschsprung disease Type II (HSCR II) and is characterized by an absence of epidermal melanocytes and enteric ganglia.
Here, we report a homozygous substitution/deletion mutation of the EDN3 gene in a WS-HSCR patient.

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants reduce endothelin-3 ligand availability, endothelin receptor type B function, or receptor-mediated signaling in neural crest-derived melanoblast and enteric precursor populations.
nodes.id: EDN3 ligand and EDNRB receptor signaling deficiency

evidence.snippet: Mutations in the genes encoding the endothelin type-B receptor (EDNRB) and its physiological ligand endothelin 3 (EDN3) are now known to account for the majority of HSCR II patients.
EDN3 thus becomes the third known gene (after RET and EDNRB) predisposing to HSCR, supporting the view that the endothelin-signaling pathways play a major role in the development of neural crests.
We recently mapped a recessive susceptibility locus (HSCR2) to human chromosome 13q22, which we now demonstrate to be the endothelin-B receptor gene (EDNRB).

nodes.description: Reduced enteric neural crest colonization leaves distal bowel regions aganglionic, producing Hirschsprung disease.

evidence.snippet: Hirschsprung's disease (HSCR) is characterized by an absence of enteric ganglia

Here, we describe a mutation of the human gene for endothelin 3 (EDN3), homozygously present in a patient with a combined Waardenburg syndrome type 2 (WS2) and HSCR phenotype (Shah-Waardenburg syndrome).

nodes.description: Heterozygous EDNRB variants can cause or contribute to WS2-like or WS4-like phenotypes with incomplete penetrance. This arm is modeled as a limited, dosage-sensitive susceptibility mechanism rather than the canonical AR WS4A mechanism.

evidence.snippet: molecular segregation investigations unraveled a dominant mode of inheritance with incomplete penetrance.
We now estimate EDNRB mutations to be responsible for 5%-6% of WS2.

nodes.description: Hypopigmented skin or hair patches and ocular pigmentary findings reflect melanoblast and melanocyte developmental failure.

evidence.snippet: characterized by an absence of epidermal melanocytes and enteric ganglia.

nodes.description: Sensorineural hearing loss is part of the Waardenburg component of the EDN3/EDNRB axis phenotype.

evidence.snippet: This condition is associated with sensorineural hearing loss; atypical pigmentation of the hair, skin, and eyes; and Hirschsprung’s disease (aganglionic megacolon).

nodes.description: Melanocyte deficiency in the epidermis, hair, iris, and cochlear stria vascularis produces the pigmentary and sensorineural hearing components of Waardenburg-Shah syndrome.
nodes.id: Stria vascularis and cutaneous melanocyte deficiency

evidence.snippet: double mutants present with a severe increase in white spotting, absence of melanocytes within the inner ear, and in the stria vascularis in particular, and more severe ENS defects.

nodes.description: EDNRB-mediated signaling is required during a critical embryonic window for terminal migration of melanoblast and enteric neuron precursors, rather than for their initial specification from the neural crest.
nodes.id: Terminal migration defect of melanoblast and enteric precursors

evidence.snippet: EDNRB signaling is exclusively required between E10.5 and E12.5 during the migratory phase of melanoblast and enteric neuroblast development.
